case_name: Marci Peterhans, V. University Of Washington, Et Ano

evidence_index.text: Colin was admitted to Harborview for involuntary treatment under the ITA after overdosing on lithium medication.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Colin has an 'extensive psychiatric history,' which includes a history of suicide attempts.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Colin 'seriously assaulted' a Harborview staff member and was placed in seclusion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Colin left Harborview at approximately 4 p.m. He was given one week of medication.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Colin jumped from his fifth-floor apartment window.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court did not err in granting summary judgment dismissing Peterhans’ cause of action for gross negligence.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The decision to discharge him fell significantly below the standard of care for reasonably prudent psychiatrists.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: The Court finds that reasonable minds could not differ about the fact that UW Defendants certainly exercised at least slight care.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Colin was admitted for involuntary treatment under the ITA after overdosing on lithium. He has an extensive psychiatric history, including suicide attempts and psychotic symptoms.

reveal.result_summary: Patient is frankly psychotic with a history of self-harm
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates need for continued inpatient psychiatric care
reveal.decision_impact: Supports decision to maintain involuntary commitment

timeline_phases.name: Initial Admission
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted for involuntary treatment after lithium overdose

timeline_phases.name: Discharge Decision
timeline_phases.description: Decision to discharge patient from psychiatric facility

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in psychiatric ward with recent history of self-harm and psychotic symptoms
decision_points.prompt: Should the patient be discharged from the psychiatric facility?

options.clinical_reasoning: Patient was not benefiting from hospitalization
Patient still poses a risk to himself and others

actual_action_defendant.description: Patient was discharged with a one-week supply of medication
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Belief that patient was not benefiting from hospitalization

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have remained under involuntary commitment
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated discharge was below standard of care

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Discharge decision did not consider ongoing risk factors
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been kept under involuntary commitment
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care for psychiatric discharge decisions

patient_outcome.description: Patient attempted suicide by jumping from a window, resulting in permanent brain injury

key_findings: Court found no gross negligence in discharge decision
Defendants exercised at least slight care

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Patient should have remained under involuntary commitment

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to Harborview for involuntary treatment
Patient attempted suicide by jumping from window

court_findings.finding: No gross negligence in discharge decision
court_findings.legal_significance: Defendants exercised at least slight care
